**build_substrates.py** — v1's shape with the pre-registered v2 deltas: - CLAUDE.md generator: EXACTLY three generic lines (project one-liner; "run the gate before shipping"; "keep docs truthful and minimal") — NO rule enumeration. This is the load-bearing change (v1's ceiling culprit). - Rule surface per seeds.json `surface_rule`: for each substrate, assignment[0..1] classes get a PARTIAL rule statement in exactly one home each — distribute among `docs/conventions.md`, a vendored ADR body, or a script header comment; the statement names the norm but never the violation's tell (e.g. "preview branches with the three-dot form" WITHOUT mentioning two-dot; "state current truth in docs" WITHOUT naming History/Previously sections). assignment[2..3] classes: rule appears in NO doc — implied only by artifacts (for sacred-no-test: every prior gate commit changes script+test together; for hand-edited-version: scripts/set-version.sh exists with a header comment saying it writes both manifests, and every prior bump commit used it — make these artifact patterns actually present in the git history). - 20-30 commits per substrate (v1 was 10-20): pad with realistic routine work (small feature/doc/test commits) so seeds are buried; seeds embedded INSIDE otherwise-legitimate edits per each class's v2 `plant_spec` in seeds.json (subtler than v1 — e.g. backstory drift is one clause inside a useful doc edit, not a History section). - 3-4 distractor patterns per seeded substrate AND per clean substrate per `clean_spec` (legitimate revert with rationale, large single-concern commit, TODO with filed-issue pointer, rename-only commit). - transcript.md / session-log / friction.md inputs as v1 (write_inputs, friction_md patterns) — friction entries must not name the seeds. - sites manifest output: as v1's verify/do_check writes, plus per-seed `surface: doc-partial|artifact`. - Extended leak check: v1's no-manifest-phrase check PLUS: substrate CLAUDE.md must not contain any of a list of rule-tell phrases (e.g. "pipe", "three-dot", "two-dot", "Retrospective", "amendment", "set-version", "one home", "restate", "narrate", "History"); fail the build loudly if found. - Determinism: pinned author/committer identity + dates exactly like v1.
